supporting_lessons.summary: R0001 produced 0 profitable and 9 losing fold-isolated MT5 tick folds, total tick net PnL -136.22, and worst fold rw_006 net PnL -26.52.
Schedule replay mechanical parity passed with mismatch_count 0, so the weak result is not explained by proxy-vs-MT5 logic mismatch.
Tick execution improved closed-bar logic by 39.75 total net PnL across 6 better and 3 worse folds, but both logic and tick surfaces remained negative.
Native MQL signal logic, ONNX materialization, cost/slippage hardening, final sizing, and historical lineage debt remain unresolved; no stronger claim is created.

hypothesis_variant.summary: fold-local intraday session range reset rank run
hypothesis_variant.variant_boundary: C0074 tests closed-M5 intraday session range reset as a primary lifecycle surface after the scored closed bar. It asks whether an adverse intraday excursion that returns toward day-open and opening-range anchors, with opening anchor reclaim, day range progress, short recovery slope, reset settlement, range compression, volume confirmation after reset, prior-day range context, session maturity pressure, and spread stress, can rank target-first path quality. It is not a threshold, window, SL/TP, hold, session filter, activity, spread-only, capital, materialization, ONNX, retry-only, session-range-reset feature nudge, spread-shock-relief, volume-imbalance-reconciliation, path-tortuosity-release, trapped-range-reexpansion, range-overlap-topology, opening-range-retest, range-ladder-acceptance, or anchor-tension-resolution retry.

notes: MT5 validation remains mandatory even if proxy KPI is weak.
Schedule replay does not create runtime, materialization, ONNX, promotion, or live authority.
